Package org.eventb.core.ast

Class Assignment

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.ast.Formula<Assignment>
org.eventb.core.ast.Assignment
Direct Known Subclasses:
BecomesEqualTo, BecomesMemberOf, BecomesSuchThat
public abstract class Assignment extends Formula<Assignment>
Common implementation for event-B assignments.

There are various kinds of assignments which are implemented in sub-classes of this class. The commonality between these assignments is that they are formed of two parts: a left-hand side and a right hand-side. The left-hand side, that is a list of free identifiers, is implemented in this class, while the right-hand side is implemented in subclasses.

This class is not intended to be subclassed by clients.

Since:
1.0

  • Method Details

    • getAssignedIdentifiers

      public final FreeIdentifier[] getAssignedIdentifiers()
      Return the left-hand side of this assignment.
      Returns:
      an array of the free identifiers that make up the left-hand side of this assignment

    • rewrite

      public final Assignment rewrite(IFormulaRewriter rewriter)
      Description copied from class: Formula
      Rewrites this formula using the given rewriter. The rewriting operation is performed in a depth-first, post-order traversal. This means that the formula tree is traversed depth-first and the rewriter is called for each node of the formula. The calls to the rewriter are done in post-order: children are rewritten before their parent.

      Additionaly, each time a quantified formula is traversed, method IFormulaRewriter.enteringQuantifier(int) (resp. IFormulaRewriter.leavingQuantifier(int)) is called just before (resp. after) processing the children of the quantified formula.

      If no rewrite was performed on this formula (that is all rewrite calls on sub-formulas returned an identical sub-formula), then a reference to this formula is returned (rather than a copy of this formula). This allows to test efficiently (using ==) whether rewriting made any change.

      This operation is not supported for assignments and bound identifier declarations. The returned formula is type-checked if this formula is type-checked.

      Overrides:
      rewrite in class Formula<Assignment>
      Parameters:
      rewriter - the rewriter to apply
      Returns:
      this formula with the given rewriter applied to it
      See Also:
      • IFormulaRewriter

    • getFISPredicate

      public final Predicate getFISPredicate()
      Returns the (flattened) feasibility predicate of this assignment. An exception is thrown if this assignment was not type checked.
      Returns:
      Returns the feasibility predicate
      Since:
      3.0

    • getBAPredicate

      public final Predicate getBAPredicate()
      Returns the (flattened) before-after predicate of this assignment. An exception is thrown if this assignment was not type checked.
      Returns:
      Returns the before-after predicate of this assignment
      Since:
      3.0

    • getUsedIdentifiers

      public abstract FreeIdentifier[] getUsedIdentifiers()
      Returns an array of the free identifiers that occur on the right-hand side of this assignment. The free identifiers are extracted using Formula.getFreeIdentifiers() applied to all formulas that are part of the right-hand side of this assignment.
      Returns:
      all free identifiers that occur in the right-hand side of this assignment.

    • getChild

      public final Formula<?> getChild(int index)
      Description copied from class: Formula
      Returns the child at the given index. Child indexes are counted from 0 for the first child to getChildCount() - 1. for the last one.

      This method is not applicable to assignments.

      Specified by:
      getChild in class Formula<Assignment>
      Parameters:
      index - index of the child to retrieve
      Returns:
      the child at the given index

    • getChildCount

      public final int getChildCount()
      Description copied from class: Formula
      Returns the number of children of this formula.

      This method is not applicable to assignments.

      Specified by:
      getChildCount in class Formula<Assignment>
      Returns:
      the number of children
